Najena
QuoteReply
# May 18 2009 at 7:23 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591969303466
12 posts
People still camp Najena for the robe? (For that matter, do people still sport these robes?)
▲ Page top
Najena
QuoteReply
# May 19 2009 at 4:31 PM Rating: Decent
Philliden
Scholar
200 posts
"People still camp Najena for the robe?"

I would presume so. It's needed for Necromancer Epic 1.0 and, if not available in the bazaar, they have to camp it. Even if it is available in the bazaar, that means someone else camped it. I plan to camp it for my necro instead of buying it.


"For that matter, do people still sport these robes?"

Unlikely. There is so much better now available even at level 1.

# Aug 20 2002 at 7:02 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591781695916
33 posts
Well I bought a Green Silken Drape for 75pp on my 11 Enchanter after recieving a FBR as a gift. I transed the FBR to my necro, but if I had known how great the GSD was for my necro I would still have it, but hey! Now my Necro looks sweet all in black with the design.
▲ Page top
RE: Hmmm GSD or FBR?
QuoteReply
# Oct 22 2002 at 2:28 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591765525919
Scholar
748 posts
I agree. Stat-wise, this isn't really the best robe out there for a necro, but in my opinion, has the best robe graphic of any of them. A necromancer just looks rediculous in say, a crystal covered shroud or even worse, a Robe of the Oracle! A necromancer should not have a sun on their robe. Might as well have a smily face!

I'll probably keep this robe even when better are within my grasp, and just improve my other slots. I love the way this looks!

Reason its 30
QuoteReply
# Jul 01 2002 at 2:51 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591779845481
21 posts
Im guessing the reason this robe is 30+ is that it is one of the components/parts of the necro epic quest. I'm gonna say no more as I dont want to spoil for ppl.

Malakith Darkstalker 23 DE Necro
Soddof Baldric 40 Dwarf Warrior

Vallon Zek

yes i do like pvp )
▲ Page top
RE: Reason its 30
QuoteReply
# Jul 02 2002 at 3:57 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592248745626
View User Forum
Guru
473 posts
Erm...huh?

The reason why it has "Level to Attain: 30" is because it drops from Najena herself. So you need to be at least lvl 30 to have a realistic chance of getting the Robe.

It's not that horrible "Recommended Level" that you have on some of the newer gear. "Level to Attain" is a rough estimate added on this site.
▲ Page top
RE: Reason its 30
QuoteReply
# Jul 22 2003 at 1:52 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591788855623
274 posts
Recommended level is great, in my opinion. It stops all the twinks from thinking they're invincible and taking over zones that they're half the recommended level to take!

Now, don't get me wrong, twinks are just fine in by books so long as there's two things up with them:

One: They don't go hunting in areas FAR too high for them. And yes, they can have a lot of hp and mana these days.

Two: They LISTEN when the healer says 'oom'. There's nothing I hate more than a guy who thinks he's so uber that monsters aren't damaging him, rather than figuring out it's the healer keeping him AND HIS GROUP alive, that he pulls a couple reds and gets him and everyone else killed.
▲ Page top
RE: Reason its 30
QuoteReply
# Jan 03 2008 at 1:02 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593369028900
12 posts
It is funny that 5 years after this post was written, I made a lvl 1 necro who was able to twink to 3k hps and 2.9k mana with 599 ac. I didn't die until lvl 51.

# Feb 20 2002 at 1:29 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592758914810
1 post
Amen man. I remember when i just started playing, the very second day. After a few weeks my magician was up to level 30 and i camped this thing all day in Najena. I was so proud of myself because It was uber! the only thing better than it was an Oracle robe (SMR's were pretty unheard of back than). Also before the implimentation of the secure drop item window. someone tricked me into dropping my FBR and i barely was able to pick it up! i almost cried!!!

I'm a lvl 5 necro on the Rathe. Someone just announced one day in Nektulos Forest that the first newb caster to poke him would get a good robe. I ran up, and the FBR was the robe. I love it! I'll definitely pass it on when I get an upgrade.
▲ Page top
really worth that much?
QuoteReply
# Nov 25 2001 at 12:53 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591772067971
3 posts
this dosen't look like a good robe well mabe for a newbie.But should the lvl to utain be 30?I wouldn't buy this robe for 120plat in my upion
▲ Page top
RE: really worth that much?
QuoteReply
# Dec 22 2001 at 2:14 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1618791888885
Scholar
122 posts
Back in the day, before all any of the expansions, in the old original EverQuest... the FBR was the pride of the level 30 caster. Of course, this was before the concept of twinking... when level 30 players fought level 30 mobs for what was then level 30 equipment, because you only wore that which you actually looted with your own hands. But then again, EQ was much different and much more intense game back then. Newcomers are spoiled beyond belief now with twinkage and the availability of quality equipment at dirt prices, with little or no concept of the difficulty (or, these days, lack thereof) required to obtain them. A mediocre level 10 today wears better gear than a well-equipped level 30 did back then.

3 years and 3 expansions later, the FBR isn't the best robe around any more. But that doesn't mean it's useless or worthless. People's expectations are just completely spoiled compared to the way the real EverQuest used to be many moons ago.

Just my perspective.

▲ Page top
RE: really worth that much?
QuoteReply
# Jan 04 2002 at 2:56 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592344474330
Sage
3,705 posts

# Jan 09 2002 at 3:25 AM Rating: Excellent

It's (in my opinion) a pretty nice looking robe. It has the same look as the one the Priest of Discord wears. I was going to buy one with my level 20 necromancer, not because I really needed the stats, but because I was getting tired of my Sr. Apprentice Robe and its boring appeareance. However, somebody just up and gave me a crystalline robe, and it looks pretty enough for me, so I'm sticking with that.

Really? 100-300pp?
QuoteReply
# Apr 26 2001 at 4:20 PM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Well than I'm doubly thankful to that VERY nice erudite who came out of the blue as I was recovering in front of Felwithe and simply handed it to me. I was a level 4 magician and had just completed my normal cloth armor set. He also gave me a gold bracelet that enhances some attributes (I forget which) and when I asked what I could do to repay him he said, "Just pass it on when you're done with it." Well, I certainly will and have had some of my faith restored by this unasked for act of charity!
